title: Quiz: Quantum Trajectories and Unravellings
description: Test your understanding of quantum trajectory methods and Monte Carlo wave function approaches

questions.question: What is the relationship between quantum trajectories and the master equation?

options.text: They give different predictions for system evolution
The ensemble average of trajectories equals the master equation solution
Trajectories are approximations valid only for weak coupling
Master equation is the classical limit of trajectories

questions.explanation: The ensemble average over many quantum trajectories exactly recovers the master equation evolution: rho(t) = E[|psi(t)><psi(t)|]. This is the fundamental connection between the two approaches.

questions.question: In the quantum jump method, what is the effective Hamiltonian H_eff?

options.text: H_eff = H + sum_k gamma_k L_k^dag L_k
H_eff = H - (i/2) sum_k gamma_k L_k^dag L_k

questions.explanation: The effective Hamiltonian is H_eff = H - (i*hbar/2) sum_k gamma_k L_k^dag L_k. The non-Hermitian part accounts for the probability of not having a jump.

questions.question: What physical process does the quantum jump unravelling correspond to?
questions.explanation: Quantum jumps correspond to photon counting or click detectors, where discrete detection events cause sudden state changes.

questions.question: The quantum diffusion unravelling produces continuous, smooth state evolution.
questions.explanation: True. Unlike quantum jumps which have discrete events, quantum diffusion produces continuous (though stochastic) evolution, corresponding to homodyne detection.

questions.question: What is the main computational advantage of quantum trajectories over direct master equation integration?

options.text: Trajectories work with pure states (dim d) instead of density matrices (dim d^2)
Trajectories give the exact solution in one run

questions.explanation: Quantum trajectories work with pure state vectors of dimension d instead of density matrices of dimension d^2, leading to significant memory and computational savings for large systems.

questions.question: In the quantum jump method, what determines the probability of a jump occurring in time dt?

options.text: The trace of the density matrix

questions.explanation: The jump probability depends on both the decay rate and the expectation value of L_k^dag L_k in the current state: dp_k = gamma_k dt <psi|L_k^dag L_k|psi>.

questions.question: Which statements about quantum unravellings are correct? (Select all)

options.text: Different unravellings give the same ensemble average
Individual trajectories depend on the choice of unravelling
All unravellings correspond to some physical measurement
Only quantum jumps are physically meaningful

questions.explanation: Different unravellings give the same ensemble average (the master equation solution), but individual trajectories differ. Each unravelling corresponds to a different measurement scheme on the environment (jumps = counting, diffusion = homodyne, etc.).

questions.question: After a quantum jump with operator L_k, what is the state of the system?
questions.explanation: After a jump with operator L_k, the state becomes L_k|psi> normalized: |psi'> = L_k|psi> / ||L_k|psi>||. This is the projection postulate for the jump measurement.
